title: aconitum kusnezoffii var. yamatsutae
intro: Aconitum kusnezoffii var. yamatsutae is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Aconitum kusnezoffii plant, a type of monk's hood or wolf's bane, belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include containing aconitine, a toxic alkaloid with pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects. However, due to its toxicity, it is primarily used externally to treat conditions such as eczema, skin irritations, and nerve pain, with the recommended health benefits including reduced inflammation and pain relief. Herbal preparations of Aconitum kusnezoffii var. yamatsutae are typically made from the dried root or rhizome, and are often used in traditional Chinese medicine, though its use is highly cautioned due to the risk of overdose or toxicity.
